content: WhatsApp ban risk: Meta detects unofficial tools at the protocol level; automation/bulk-sending extensions cause account bans. Oct 2025: Socket exposed 131 Chrome extensions (DBX Tecnologia franchise, mostly Brazilian market) hijacking WhatsApp Web for spam; Google removed them. Read-only DOM observation carries far lower ban risk than sending messages or touching WhatsApp internals, but sources do not document a formal safe harbor — ToS prohibits any unauthorized automated access. Mitigation: never send messages programmatically, never inject into WhatsApp's JS internals, keep footprint passive (MutationObserver only), warn users of residual risk in terms of service.
